Off Calendar Aug-03-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT FIRST STUDENT, INC'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITION ANSWERS FROM ENVIRONMENTAL LAW FOUNDATION.
Appearances: Laura J. Baughman of Baron & Budd (214.523.6687) for plaintiffs and responding parties; Joonsik Maing and Jesse Miller of Reed Smith LLP (659.4812) for defendants and moving parties.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: defendant's motion to compel deposition answers is granted in part. Plaintiff's witness must answer questions seeking the identity of witnesses who have knowledge of relevant information. While ELF does not have to provide a list of who was interviewed or identify on a wholesale basis the witnesses they interviewed, the identity of persons who have knowledge of relevant information, whether interviewed or not by ELF, is discoverable. The motion is denied as to questions seeking information about communications between ELF and Mr. Padilla or Mr. Contreras but not the specific information these gentlemen have that is relevant to the case. The court suggests defendants depose Mr. Padilla and Mr. Contreras to find out what they know. Further, the court allows defendants no more than 8 hours of deposition time as to Ms. Cappel. Defendant to submit a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Not reported
